data: Die Villa von Piazza Armerina, Residenz des kaiserlichen Prokurators, und ein mit ihr verbundenes Emporium von Henna

Giacomo Manganaro, ‘Die Villa von Piazza Armerina, Residenz des kaiserlichen Prokurators, und ein mit ihr verbundenes Emporium von Henna’, in Palast und Hütte : Beiträge zum Bauen und Wohnen im Altertum von Archäologen, Vor- und Frühgeschichtlern: Tagungsbeiträge eines Symposiums der Alexander von Humboldt-Stiftung, Bonn-Bad Godesberg, veranstaltet vom 25.-30. November 1979 in Berlin, ed. D. Papenfuss and V.M. Strocka (Mainz: Philipp von Zabern, 1982), 493–513

Beginnings of six lines preserved; in Bernabo Brea's transcription the left margin is irregular, suggesting the lines were centred.
Brea notes that the letters tend towards cursive.
2nd — 3rd century CE (?) (roman, imperial, official, tending to the 'cursive')

Text fills the plaque, centered heading, consistent left margin, appears compressed in the final lines.

standard alpha, epsilon in form of four-bar sigma, with extended middle bar; theta rhomboid; cursive mu; rhomboid omicron; sigma of standard four-bar form; rhomboid phi; uncial omega. Some use of interpuncts and supralines.

Parts of five evenly spaced regularly inscribed lines of Greek letters
Alpha with straight bar, lunate epsilon, uncial omega, cursive mu, kappa with short bars; pronounced terminal serifs of the wedge variety.
Manganaro, 1994, Iscrizioni, epigrafi ed epigrammi in Greco della Sicilia orientale di epoca romana
